headline: Cleveland Fed floats rate hikes on AI-driven inflation
detail: Fed President Hammack said AI could fuel inflation and that rate hikes may be necessary. This directly pressures high-multiple tech and semiconductor names - exactly your 90% concentration. On margin, a rate-driven selloff compounds losses faster than a cash account.
rec: No specific new buy warranted. Consider trimming the most leveraged/extended position (SOXL) toward the $220-230 zone if this narrative gains any traction; it has no earnings cushion against rising rates.

headline: Wall Street bulls worried about post-Q2 pullback
detail: Even bullish strategists are flagging that the strong second quarter has stretched valuations and set up a potential pullback. For a margin account concentrated in tech/chips, a mean-reversion pullback hits harder than it looks on paper.
rec: No new buys into this. Use this as a prompt to audit your stop levels across SMH, SOXL, and QQQ - make sure stops are current, not set-and-forgotten.

headline: AeroVironment surges 15% on defense spending surge
detail: Defense drone names are flying on the Trump budget headlines. You have no direct defense exposure, but this signals sector rotation money moving toward defense and away from pure tech - a headwind for concentration risk in your book.
rec: No position in AVAV or defense names and no scanner setup aligns here - do not chase a 15% gap move. Noted as a rotation signal to monitor, nothing more.

headline: Japanese yen sinks to 40-year low, intervention risk rising
detail: A weak yen and potential Bank of Japan intervention can trigger sharp global risk-off moves, as seen in August 2024. That kind of sudden unwind hits leveraged and margin accounts in tech/semis disproportionately fast.
rec: No trade directly from this, but keep it on radar - a surprise BoJ move or intervention announcement could be the external shock that breaks the tech rally. Make sure your stops are live, not just mental.
